title: ED patient declined care documentation

red_flags.text: Supports documenting triage, current self-harm or suicide risk, leaving risk, capacity for a decision to leave, prior psychiatric history, mental-state examination and referral or follow-up when performed.
red_flags.rationale: When mental illness, self-harm or leaving risk is relevant, the exact RCEM section supports recording immediate safety, capacity for the decision to leave, specialist discussion and handover only when assessed or arranged.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports documenting triage, current self-harm or suicide risk, leaving risk, capacity for a decision to leave, prior psychiatric history, mental-state examination and referral or follow-up when performed.

source.title: Mental Health in Emergency Departments: A toolkit for improving care

exact_section.heading: Mental-health audit standards for the emergency department

assessment.text: Supports a time- and decision-specific capacity assessment, presumption of capacity, practical support, and recording the assessment; UK legal rules require UAE adaptation.
assessment.rationale: The exact capacity section supports a focused, time- and decision-specific assessment only when completed; it does not auto-state capacity.
assessment.evidence_extract: Supports a time- and decision-specific capacity assessment, presumption of capacity, practical support, and recording the assessment; UK legal rules require UAE adaptation.

exact_section.heading: Paragraphs 76–86 — If a patient may lack capacity

treatment_and_management.text: Supports recording the exchange of information leading to a decision, the decision and action agreed, who made or agreed it, and a decision to take no action.
treatment_and_management.rationale: The exact GMC recording section supports documenting the patient’s concerns or goals, information exchange, decision, agreed or declined action, decision-maker, completed risk discussion and clinician plan; it does not override UAE law.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports recording the exchange of information leading to a decision, the decision and action agreed, who made or agreed it, and a decision to take no action.

sources_used.title: Decision making and consent
sources_used.jurisdiction: United Kingdom professional standards; UAE consent and capacity law governs local use

sources_used.title: Mental Health in Emergency Departments: A toolkit for improving care
sources_used.jurisdiction: United Kingdom emergency medicine; UAE law and pathways govern local use

source_limitations: Specific care declined, information provided, alternatives, comprehension, capacity elements, legal authority, surrogate, immediate risk, departure circumstances and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
